mission.title: kubescape: Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.
mission.description: Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check Kubescape installation
steps.description:
Verify your Kubescape installation:
```bash
kubescape version
```
Install if needed: `curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubescape/kubescape/master/install.sh | /bin/bash`. Kubescape can run as a CLI tool or be deployed in-cluster.

steps.title: Review kubescape configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubescape configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape
kubectl get configmap -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubescape
```
Kubernetes offers a simple NetworkPolicy CRD but is limited in functionality and doesn't completely provide all the necessary controls and policy capabilities that most CNIs would. This is a base functionality.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.
steps.description:
Yes, but I am not sure what controls. Do you mean those that check if a
workload has a bound network policy?

On Mon, Feb 12, 2024 at 7:34 AM David Wertenteil ***@***.***>
wrote:

>  <https://github.com/slashben> Yossi has generating cilium and
> calico NP up his sleeve. Maybe we should add the
```yaml
apiVersion: "cilium.io/v2"
kind: CiliumNetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all
  namespace: default
spec:
  endpointSelector:
    matchLabels: {}
  ingress: []
  egress: []
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape
kubectl get events -n kubescape --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Yes, but I am not sure what controls. Do you mean those that check if a
workload has a bound network policy?

On Mon, Feb 12, 2024 at 7:34 AM David Wertenteil ***@***.***>
wrote:

>  <https://github.com/slashben> Yossi has generating cilium and
> calico NP up his sleeve. Maybe we should add the controls support as part
> of that epic.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: "cilium.io/v2"
kind: CiliumNetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all
  namespace: default
spec:
  endpointSelector:
    matchLabels: {}
  ingress: []
  egress: []

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubescape installed or the issue environment reproducible.
